content: Staging requires comprehensive diagnostic workup. Imaging: CT chest/abdomen/pelvis for most solid tumors; MRI for brain, rectal, prostate cancers; PET-CT for lymphoma, lung cancer, melanoma; bone scan for prostate, breast cancer with symptoms. Pathology: biopsy confirms diagnosis, provides histologic grade; surgical specimens determine pathologic stage (pTNM). Endoscopy: colonoscopy for colorectal cancer, bronchoscopy for lung cancer, EGD for GI cancers. Bone marrow biopsy for hematologic malignancies. Biomarkers: PSA for prostate, CA-125 for ovarian, CEA for colorectal monitoring. Molecular testing: EGFR/ALK for lung cancer, HER2 for breast, KRAS/NRAS for colorectal, BRAF for melanoma. Clinical stage (cTNM) based on pre-treatment evaluation; pathologic stage (pTNM) after surgery; restaging (rTNM) for recurrence. AJCC 8th edition incorporates molecular markers for breast, prostate, thyroid cancers into prognostic stage groups.
